document.title: Risk Management Report for A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW

sourceSummary.oneLine: Risk management report for H&abyz Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports technical documentation by documenting risk management activities, residual risk acceptability, and production/post-production information review for the device.
keyPoints: The device is a Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector with Classification IIb and expected service life of approximately 5 years.
Applicable regulations listed include Regulation (EU) 2017/745 and the Medical Device Act in Korea.
Risk acceptability criteria define acceptable risk levels as 1 to 4 and unacceptable risk levels as 5 to 25.
Before risk control, 43 risks were identified: 2 acceptable and 41 unacceptable.
After risk control, all 43 residual risks were evaluated in the acceptable region.

Production and post-production information is collected through customer satisfaction surveys, complaint monitoring, inspection monitoring, design changes, standards monitoring, market information, process monitoring, and purchaser assessment monitoring.

entities.name: H&abyz Co., Ltd.
entities.evidence: This is a document for the H&abyz Co., Ltd. to establish and document a risk management plan in accordance with the risk management procedure.

entities.name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
entities.evidence: Product name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

entities.name: European Union
entities.evidence: European Union Regulation (EU) 2017/745 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 5 April 2017

entities.name: Republic of Korea
entities.evidence: Republic of Korea Medical Device Act in Korea

entities.name: Quality manager
entities.evidence: According to the seriousness of the information collected, the Quality manager shall report safety information and any side effects to the regulatory agency

concepts.name: Risk management
concepts.evidence: systematic application of management policies, procedures (3.13) and practices to the tasks of analysing, evaluating, controlling and monitoring risk (3.18)

concepts.name: Risk management file
concepts.evidence: set of records (3.16) and other documents that are produced by risk management (3.24)

concepts.name: Risk acceptability criteria
concepts.evidence: Acceptable Risk (A): Acceptable Region, Green Zone, Risk levels: 1~4 Unacceptable Risk (U): Unacceptable Region, Red Zone, Risk levels: 5~25

concepts.name: Overall residual risk
concepts.evidence: Finally, the overall residual risks are determined to be acceptable through the warning, operating method and adverse event reporting.

concepts.name: Production and post-production information
concepts.evidence: activities related to collection and review of relevant production and post-production information.

concepts.name: Intended purpose
concepts.evidence: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

concepts.name: Reasonably foreseeable misuse
concepts.evidence: Might be misused by maintenance and calibration error of operator.

concepts.name: Essential performance
concepts.evidence: Manufacturer shall identify which functions are Essential Performance.

concepts.name: Benefit-risk analysis
concepts.evidence: The benefit-risk analysis is used to determine if the residual risk is outweighed by the expected benefits of the intended use of the medical device.

evidence.claim: The document is Risk Management Report Rev. 5 dated April 22, 2024.
evidence.evidenceText: Technical Documentation Document No. A-TCF-5-104 Rev. No. 5 Risk Management Report Rev. Date April 22, 2024

evidence.claim: The report covers Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.
evidence.evidenceText: Product name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Model name: A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW

evidence.claim: The device classification stated in the report is IIb.
evidence.evidenceText: Product Name Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Model Name A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW Classification IIb Expected service life Approximately 5 years

evidence.claim: The expected service life is approximately 5 years.
evidence.evidenceText: Classification IIb Expected service life Approximately 5 years

evidence.claim: Applicable regulations include Regulation (EU) 2017/745 and the Medical Device Act in Korea.
evidence.evidenceText: European Union Regulation (EU) 2017/745 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 5 April 2017 Republic of Korea Medical Device Act in Korea

evidence.claim: EN ISO 14971:2019 is listed as a relevant standard for risk management.
evidence.evidenceText: EN ISO 14971:2019 Medical devices - Application of risk management to medical devices (ISO 14971:2019)

evidence.claim: The intended purpose is general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

evidence.claim: The device is not to be used for mammography, infants, pregnant women, fluoroscopy, or angiography.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography. Not to be used for infant and pregnant women. Not to be used for fluoroscopy and angiography.

evidence.claim: The device is a wired or wireless digital flat panel detector with TFT and CsI scintillator.
evidence.evidenceText: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW are wired or wireless digital flat panel detectors... The detector consists of TFT and Scintillator (CsI)

evidence.claim: The risk analysis process includes intended use, safety-related characteristics, hazards and hazardous situations, and risk estimation.
evidence.evidenceText: The risk analysis process consists of the following steps... description of the intended use... identification of the characteristics... identification of hazards and hazardous situations... estimation of risks

evidence.claim: Acceptable risk is defined as risk levels 1 to 4, and unacceptable risk as levels 5 to 25.
evidence.evidenceText: Acceptable Risk (A): Acceptable Region, Green Zone, Risk levels: 1~4 Unacceptable Risk (U): Unacceptable Region, Red Zone, Risk levels: 5~25

evidence.claim: Before risk control, 43 risks were identified, with 2 acceptable and 41 unacceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Through the identification of known and foreseeable hazards, 43 risks were identified and estimated... Acceptable region: 2 ea Unacceptable region: 41 ea

evidence.claim: After risk control, all 43 identified residual risks were in the acceptable region.
evidence.evidenceText: All identified residual risks evaluated after implementation of the risk control measures, are distributed in an acceptable region... EN ISO 14971:2019 43 0 43

evidence.claim: Risk control measures included labeling, user manual, certified battery, IEC 60601-1 testing, cable differentiation, IEC 62304 software process, radio-related testing, and certified RF module.
evidence.evidenceText: As control measures for the identified risks, providing label and user manual, using certified battery, testing the device to meet IEC 60601-1, designing each cable type differently, establishing software development process in accordance with IEC 62304, radio-related testing, and using certified RF module, etc. were implemented and verified.

evidence.claim: Overall residual risks were determined acceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Finally, the overall residual risks are determined to be acceptable through the warning, operating method and adverse event reporting.

evidence.claim: The report refers to A-TCF-5-161 Post-Market Surveillance for production and post-production information.
evidence.evidenceText: Refer to A-TCF-5-161 Post-Market Surveillance for getting the information of production and post- production phases.

evidence.claim: Quality Assurance collects customer satisfaction survey information once every 12 months.
evidence.evidenceText: Customer satisfaction survey - Conduct the activities such as maintenance and repair at customer site, once every 12 months in accordance with the feedback procedure

evidence.claim: The Quality manager is responsible for annual risk analysis of collected production and post-production information.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk analysis for the collected information (once every 12 months)

reviewFlags.reason: Authority metadata indicates MFDS/domestic, but the document lists both European Union Regulation (EU) 2017/745 and Republic of Korea Medical Device Act in Korea.
The notified body is referenced only as 3EC (NB); the full legal name is not present in the source text.
Some OCR or source text quality issues are present, including apparent misspellings and inconsistent standard references such as EN 15322-1 where ISO 15223-1 is also listed.
